scenarios.summary: Automated review, reviewer artifact retry, and review-budget split behavior are extension proof outside source-core certification.
proofCommands: npm test -- tests/review-retry.test.ts tests/review.test.ts tests/review-budget.test.ts --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: CI diagnostics, landing gates, branch freshness, and GitHub merge shepherd behavior are high-trust extension proof.
proofCommands: npm test -- tests/landing-policy.test.ts tests/landing-preflight.test.ts tests/github.test.ts --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: Monitor snapshot assembly, launcher, static dashboard shell, and read-only API routes remain extension-owned.
proofCommands: npm test -- tests/http-server.test.ts tests/monitor-aggregator.test.ts tests/monitor-launcher.test.ts tests/dashboard-profiler-ui.test.ts --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: Registry-wide fair scheduling, project locks, and transient project errors are optional coordinator behavior.
Planning/decomposition helpers and child/follow-up Linear issue creation stay outside source-core dispatch proof.
Report-only and applied model-routing decisions are optimization telemetry, not source-core execution policy.
Raw Linear GraphQL remains a client-tool extension available only through explicit workflow opt-in.

evidence.testName: advertises only for agent-owned Linear workflows with auth
returns structured availability and unsupported-tool failures
advertises agent-owned linear_graphql and answers unsupported client tool calls

proofCommands: npm test -- tests/linear-graphql-tool.test.ts tests/runner.test.ts -t "advertises only for agent-owned Linear workflows with auth|returns structured availability and unsupported-tool failures|advertises agent-owned linear_graphql and answers unsupported client tool calls" --reporter verbose

scenarios.summary: Tracker adapters beyond the Linear default use the adapter registry and do not expand source-core certification.
